changed.from: Under ass:positivity-floor and ass:budget-balance, the feasible set P_{n,B_n,epsilon} is a nonempty compact convex subset of [0,1]^{m_n}, the map p -> V_env(G_n,p) is convex on P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, and therefore the argmin set in def:optimal-design is nonempty. Every minimizer p_n^*(G_n) satisfies the observable KKT system g_k(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) = lambda_n + nu_{k,n}^+ - nu_{k,n}^- for each k in I_n together with nu_{k,n}^+((p_n^*(G_n))_k-(1-epsilon)) = 0 and nu_{k,n}^-(epsilon-(p_n^*(G_n))_k) = 0.
changed.to: Under ass:positivity-floor and ass:budget-balance, the feasible set P_{n,B_n,epsilon} is a nonempty compact convex subset of [0,1]^{m_n}, the map p -> V_env(G_n,p) is convex on P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, and therefore the argmin set in def:optimal-design is nonempty. Every minimizer p_n^*(G_n) satisfies the observable KKT system g_k(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) = lambda_n - nu_{k,n}^+ + nu_{k,n}^- for each k in I_n together with nu_{k,n}^+((p_n^*(G_n))_k-(1-epsilon)) = 0 and nu_{k,n}^-(epsilon-(p_n^*(G_n))_k) = 0.
changed.reason: The displayed KKT signs for the box multipliers are reversed. For a minimization over epsilon<=p_k<=1-epsilon with a fixed budget, an active upper-bound coordinate can only have g_k at or below the equality multiplier, while the current formula g_k=lambda+nu^+-nu^- forces it at or above. The corrected normal-cone certificate is g_k=lambda-nu^+ + nu^-; the compactness, convexity, and existence parts remain valid and are proved in lem:envelope-program-correct-kkt.

note: maximality improvement injection (round 2): add separation theorem + bounded-degree surrogate certificate
directive:
MAXIMALITY IMPROVEMENT DIRECTIVE (orchestrator, round 2 — re-solve D0). The paper is already fully proved; this round ADDS two new results that complete the promised field-tier contribution. Both MUST be derived under the EXISTING frozen assumptions ONLY (ass:bipartite-interference, ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, ass:bounded-outcomes, ass:bounded-outcome-degree, ass:bounded-overlap-dependency, ass:positivity-floor, ass:budget-balance, ass:variance-nondegenerate). Do NOT introduce any new assumption to force either result, do NOT weaken or restate any existing theorem, and do NOT gerrymander a hypothesis to encode the conclusion — a nonstandard or tautological hypothesis is laundering and will be rejected at D0.5. Keep every existing theorem/lemma intact; ADD to the note.

ADD THEOREM A — degree-heterogeneity SEPARATION theorem (this is the promised kernel piece currently missing). Statement: fix the homogeneous budget rho = B_n / m_n and suppose rho in (epsilon, 1-epsilon) with rho != 1/2. Consider the homogeneous feasible design p^hom with all coordinates equal to rho. If the homogeneous-point overlap-gradient scores { g_k(G_n, p^hom) : k in I_n } (def:kkt-gradient, evaluated at p^hom) are NOT all equal, then (i) p^hom is NOT a minimizer of V_env over P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, hence every envelope-optimal p_n^*(G_n) is non-homogeneous; and (ii) V_env(G_n, p_n^*(G_n)) is STRICTLY below min over homogeneous feasible designs of V_env, with an EXPLICIT observable lower bound on the gap V_env(p^hom) - V_env(p_n^*) derived from the spread of the gradient scores (a first-order descent step along the budget-feasible direction that moves mass from high-score to low-score units, controlled by the envelope's Hessian bound on the box). Prove it via: unequal gradient scores => the projected gradient of V_env onto the budget hyperplane is nonzero at p^hom => a feasible descent direction exists (strict decrease by convexity/first-order optimality, which p^hom therefore fails), and quantify the decrease by a standard quadratic-upper-bound / smoothness argument using the second derivatives already computed in thm:convex-design's proof. ADD a clean COROLLARY for singleton-exposure graphs (each outcome unit's neighborhood is a single intervention unit, so S_{ij} are singletons and the per-unit scores reduce to the observable degree summaries s_k): when the s_k^2 (equivalently h_k) are DISPERSED (not all equal), the separation hypothesis holds, so the optimal design is non-homogeneous and strictly better — the concrete named degree-dispersion regime. Keep the corollary's condition as an interpretable observable graph-dispersion statement, NOT "assume the gradient scores differ" restated.

ADD THEOREM B — POSITIVE observable surrogate certificate under the paper's own sparse regime (complements, does NOT replace, the existing negative oeq:dispersion-certificate). Keep oeq:dispersion-certificate and its proved NEGATIVE answer for first-order dispersion summaries exactly as is. ADD a theorem that, under the EXISTING ass:bounded-outcome-degree (which already bounds every shared-neighborhood size |S_{ij}(G_n)| <= dbar since S_{ij} is a subset of a single outcome unit's neighborhood), the product envelope is uniformly comparable to the additive degree-surrogate objective: prove the sandwich, for every feasible p, surrogate-objective(p) <= V_env(G_n,p)/4 <= epsilon^{-(dbar-1)} * surrogate-objective(p), where the surrogate is the h_k-weighted additive objective of def:surrogate-design (each nonempty S contributes prod_{k in S} p_k^{-1} + prod_{k in S}(1-p_k)^{-1}, bounded above by epsilon^{-(|S|-1)} times its largest single-factor term and below by that term, using epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon and |S| <= dbar). Conclude the observable approximation-ratio certificate alpha_cert(G_n) <= epsilon^{-(dbar-1)} for the degree-surrogate design p_n^{deg}(G_n). This turns the honest open frontier into a proved positive constant-factor certificate valid in the sparse bounded-degree regime the paper already assumes. State clearly in the note's scope that the certificate is a bounded-degree (dbar-dependent) guarantee, and that the first-order-dispersion-only certificate remains impossible (the negative oeq result), so the two results are complementary, not contradictory.

After adding A and B: update the Honest-scope section to reflect that the separation theorem and the bounded-degree surrogate certificate are now PROVED (theorem-level), and that what remains open is only a dbar-FREE (uniform-in-degree) constant-factor surrogate certificate. Re-run the reduction/consumer justifications for the two new results. Verify prop:homogeneous-reduction and all existing theorems still hold unchanged.

changed.to: Under ass:positivity-floor and ass:budget-balance, set rho = B_n / m_n and let p^hom denote the homogeneous feasible design with (p^hom)_k = rho for every k in I_n; assume rho in (epsilon, 1-epsilon) and rho != 1/2. If the homogeneous-point envelope-gradient scores {g_k(G_n, p^hom) : k in I_n} of def:kkt-gradient are not all equal, then p^hom is not a minimizer of p -> V_env(G_n,p) over P_{n,B_n,epsilon}; consequently every envelope-optimal design p_n^*(G_n) of def:optimal-design is non-homogeneous and satisfies the strict inequality V_env(G_n, p_n^*(G_n)) < V_env(G_n, p^hom), and the gap V_env(G_n,p^hom) - V_env(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) is bounded below by a strictly positive quantity determined observably by the spread of the gradient scores {g_k(G_n,p^hom)} together with the envelope's second-order modulus on P_{n,B_n,epsilon}. In particular, for singleton-exposure graphs, in which each outcome unit's neighborhood is a single intervention unit so that every nonempty S_{ij}(G_n) is a singleton, the gradient scores reduce to observable per-unit degree summaries, and the hypothesis (hence the strict improvement of the optimal heterogeneous design over the homogeneous design) holds whenever those summaries are not all equal.
changed.reason: maximality: add promised degree-heterogeneity separation theorem

changed.to: Under ass:positivity-floor, ass:budget-balance, and ass:bounded-outcome-degree — the last of which forces max_{i,j in O_n} |S_{ij}(G_n)| <= dbar — there is a finite constant C(epsilon, dbar) depending only on epsilon and dbar, of order epsilon^{-(dbar-1)}, such that the observable approximation ratio of def:approximation-ratio satisfies alpha_cert(G_n) <= C(epsilon, dbar). The bound follows from a uniform sandwich, valid for every feasible p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, between the graph-only envelope V_env(G_n,p) of def:graph-envelope and the additive degree-surrogate objective sum_{k in I_n} h_k(G_n){p_k^{-1} + (1-p_k)^{-1}} minimized in def:surrogate-design, in which the per-pair product loads over the shared-neighborhood sets S_{ij}(G_n) are comparable to their additive per-coordinate counterparts up to a multiplicative factor controlled by the bounded shared-neighborhood size dbar and the positivity floor epsilon. This is the positive counterpart, in the paper's own sparse bounded-degree regime, of the negative first-order-summary answer proved in oeq:dispersion-certificate.
changed.reason: maximality: add positive bounded-degree surrogate certificate (complements the negative oeq)

note: orchestrator maximality improvement: added 2 target theorems to proto (A separation, B surrogate certificate)
directive: MAXIMALITY improvement (round 3): TWO new target theorems were added to the frozen proto by the orchestrator — thm:heterogeneity-separation and thm:surrogate-certificate. PROVE each from the frozen primitives ONLY (no new assumption beyond the ones each cites; do NOT weaken or restate any existing result; reuse the 7 already-proved statements). For thm:heterogeneity-separation: unequal homogeneous-point gradient scores => nonzero budget-projected gradient at p^hom => p^hom fails first-order optimality of the convex program (thm:convex-design), so it is not a minimizer and the optimum is strictly better; quantify the strict gap by a standard smoothness/second-order descent bound using the Hessian control already in thm:convex-design's proof; derive the singleton-exposure corollary by reducing the gradient scores to the observable degree summaries. For thm:surrogate-certificate: prove the per-pair sandwich between the product envelope loads (def:graph-envelope) and the additive h_k-surrogate objective (def:surrogate-design) using epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon and |S_{ij}| <= dbar (from ass:bounded-outcome-degree), then chain through def:approximation-ratio using that p_n^{deg} minimizes the surrogate; derive the SHARPEST constant you can (order epsilon^{-(dbar-1)}). Do NOT gerrymander a hypothesis to encode either conclusion (anti-laundering). KEEP oeq:dispersion-certificate and its negative answer intact — the two are complementary. Update the honest-scope prose so the separation theorem and bounded-degree surrogate certificate are stated as PROVED, leaving only a dbar-free (uniform-in-degree) certificate open.

note: D0.5 convergence: tighten redundant assumptions to minimal faithful sets (round 4)
directive:
D0.5 CONVERGENCE FIX (round 4): the math panel flagged redundant_assumption findings — theorems/lemmas listing hypotheses their proofs never consume. The 9 top-level frozen statements have ALREADY been tightened in the proto to their minimal faithful assumption sets. Re-prove them from the tightened statements (proofs are unchanged since the removed assumptions were never used), and apply the SAME minimality discipline to EVERY helper lemma you emit. This is REMOVAL of unused hypotheses (it GENERALIZES each result) — never ADD or strengthen an assumption (that would be laundering); keep every assumption a proof actually uses.

GENERAL RULE for the minimal assumption set of any node:
- ass:positivity-floor (epsilon<=p_k<=1-epsilon) and ass:budget-balance (sum_k p_k = B_n) are REDUNDANT on any node quantified over p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon} (or over p_n^*(G_n), p_n^{deg}(G_n), which live in P). The box floor and the budget are already supplied by feasible-set membership (def:feasible-designs) together with the FROZEN SYMBOL FACTS epsilon in (0,1/2) and B_n in [m_n epsilon, m_n(1-epsilon)]. Do NOT list them as hypotheses on such nodes; get epsilon-bounds and the budget from p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon} and the symbol declarations.
- ass:bipartite-interference is needed ONLY where the estimator's exposure-consistency (T_i Y_i^obs = T_i Y_i^1, C_i Y_i^obs = C_i Y_i^0) is invoked — i.e. the Hajek linearization and any result that depends on it. It is NOT needed in purely algebraic covariance / envelope identities that manipulate Y_i^1, Y_i^0 as fixed centered scalars.
- ass:bounded-outcomes only where a boundedness step (|Y|<=1, |centered|<=2) is actually used; ass:bounded-outcome-degree only where |S_{ij}|<=dbar or pi_i>=epsilon^{dbar} is used; ass:bounded-overlap-dependency only where the degree-Dbar dependency graph is used; ass:variance-nondegenerate only where liminf sigma^2>0 is used.

Per-helper-lemma minimal keep sets (drop all others):
- lem:eta-local-dependence-and-bound: {independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, bounded-outcomes, bounded-outcome-degree, bounded-overlap-dependency}
- lem:hajek-denominator-concentration: {independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, bounded-outcome-degree, bounded-overlap-dependency}
- lem:hajek-linearization: {bipartite-interference, independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, bounded-outcomes, bounded-outcome-degree, bounded-overlap-dependency}
- lem:envelope-program-correct-kkt: {} (none — it is about P_{n,B_n,epsilon} itself; nonemptiness from B_n's frozen range)
- lem:feasible-objective-attainment: {} (none — nonemptiness from B_n's frozen range; continuity/finite inverses from p in P)
- lem:product-additive-envelope-sandwich: {bounded-outcome-degree} (the epsilon-floor comes from p in P; |S|<=dbar from bounded-outcome-degree)
- lem:centered-potential-outcome-bound: {bounded-outcomes}; lem:shared-neighborhood-size-bound: {bounded-outcome-degree}; lem:large-independent-set-bounded-degree and lem:bounded-degree-dependency-clt: {} (abstract, no ass:).

Keep all def:/lem:/thm: dependencies as before. Update each affected node's "Under ass:..." preamble to list exactly its kept assumptions (drop the redundant ones from the prose too, matching depends_on). Do not change any math content, construction, bound, or conclusion. Also refresh the Honest-scope prose to reflect that thm:heterogeneity-separation and thm:surrogate-certificate are PROVED (leaving only a dbar-free uniform-in-degree certificate open).

note: D0.5 fix round 5: total def:approximation-ratio + Doudchenko positioning
directive:
D0.5 CONVERGENCE FIX (round 5). Two defects, both fixable without changing any math result. Do NOT weaken any theorem; keep all proved content.

FIX 1 — def:approximation-ratio is now TOTAL (already edited in the frozen proto): alpha_cert(G_n) equals the ratio V_env(p_n^{deg})/min_p V_env when the envelope minimum is positive, and equals 1 when the envelope minimum is 0 (no-loss convention — on a zero-optimum graph V_env vanishes for every feasible design, so the surrogate incurs no relative loss). Re-prove thm:surrogate-certificate against this TOTAL definition: the zero-optimum case is now DEFINED as alpha_cert=1 in the core, so remove the "silent 0/0 convention" language from the proof and instead invoke the definition's zero-optimum branch directly; the bound alpha_cert(G_n) <= max{1, epsilon^{-(dbar-1)}} then holds on the whole admitted bounded-degree graph class (positive-optimum graphs via the product-additive sandwich, zero-optimum graphs via the definition's value 1). Surface the totality in the deliverable: state in thm:surrogate-certificate that alpha_cert is a total [1,infty)-valued observable.

FIX 2 — thm:convex-design positioning (novelty framing) is too thin because it omits the closest bipartite-design comparator. Add Doudchenko et al. (2020), "Causal inference with bipartite experiments/designs", arXiv:2010.02108, to the bibliography, and revise thm:convex-design's positioning/gap so the "no checked result solves ..." claim explicitly distinguishes that comparator: Doudchenko et al. (2020) study bipartite experiments through a linear exposure-response MSE objective for LINEAR estimators and do not derive a design-based conservative Hajek-variance ENVELOPE, its CONVEX minimization, or an observable GLOBAL KKT certificate for the heterogeneous-Bernoulli design; and Harshaw et al. (2021, already cited) is likewise linear-exposure-response, not a graph-only conservative-envelope design. Keep the differentiation to the same estimand/inferential question (design-based variance of the bipartite Hajek estimator). This is a POSITIONING / literature-review change only — do not alter thm:convex-design's statement, proof, or conclusion.

Everything else (the tightened minimal assumption sets from round 4, all 9 results, the separation theorem, the CLT, the coverage theorem) is correct and must be preserved unchanged.

(2020, arXiv:2010.02108) to the bibliography and distinguishes it (plus Harshaw et al. 2021) in thm:convex-design's positioning: those study bipartite experiments only via a linear exposure-response MSE for linear estimators, not the design-based conservative Hajek-variance envelope, its convex minimization, or an observable global KKT certificate. Re-prove every statement UNCHANGED (reuse all carried proofs) and re-render the note so the updated positioning/bibliography and the total-definition wording appear. Do not alter any statement, construction, bound, proof, or conclusion. Confirm thm:surrogate-certificate invokes the definition's zero-optimum branch (no silent 0/0 convention).

note: D0.5 fix round 7: 3 statement-level corrections (KKT existential, measure, oeq domain)
directive:
D0.5 CONVERGENCE FIX (round 7) — three narrow-to-truth STATEMENT corrections already applied to the frozen proto; re-prove and re-render, NO math changes, keep all proofs:
1. thm:convex-design: the KKT multipliers are now EXISTENTIALLY quantified ("there exist lambda_n, nu^+ , nu^- >= 0 such that ..."). Your proof already CONSTRUCTS these multipliers from the normal cone, so this is exactly what it proves — re-prove unchanged.
2. thm:postdesign-wald: the coverage measure is corrected from the free P_p to P_{p_n^*(G_n)} (the assignment law of the SELECTED optimal design), which is the only law the CLT/estimator objects support. Re-prove: your proof already works under the optimal design's law.
3. oeq:dispersion-certificate: the antecedent now carries the domain guard sum_{ell} s_ell^2 > 0 (so the displayed dispersion ratios are well-defined). Your negative-answer counterexample already constructs graphs with sum_{ell} s_ell^2 > 0, so it still applies verbatim.
Do not alter any construction, bound, proof, or conclusion. Re-render the note so the corrected statements appear.

directive:

DROP ass:positivity-floor. In the proof, obtain pi_i^1(p), pi_i^0(p) >= epsilon^{dbar} and |A_i-1|,|B_i-1| <= epsilon^{-dbar} from the hypothesis "p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}" via def:feasible-designs (which supplies epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon), NOT from ass:positivity-floor.

- lem:product-additive-envelope-sandwich: assumptions = {ass:bounded-outcome-degree}. DROP ass:positivity-floor AND ass:budget-balance; the lemma already quantifies p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, whose def:feasible-designs supplies both the floor and the budget.

Update each lemma's "Under ass:..." preamble to list exactly its kept assumptions, and cite "p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon} (def:feasible-designs)" wherever the proof previously cited ass:positivity-floor or ass:budget-balance. This is REMOVAL of unused hypotheses (it generalizes each lemma); never add or strengthen an assumption. Do not change any construction, bound, proof step, or conclusion. Re-render the note.

note: D0.5 root fix round 11: normalized-gradient label + wholesale positioning
directive: D0.5 ROOT FIX (round 11) — presentation/metadata only, NO math change. Two root-level fixes are applied to the frozen proto: (1) def:kkt-gradient is now labeled the gradient of the NORMALIZED envelope V_env/4 and its construction states g_k = partial_{p_k}(V_env(G_n,p)/4) explicitly (the formula is unchanged; this removes the factor-of-4 normalization-label mismatch, since every downstream proof already uses the /4-normalized gradient). (2) thm:convex-design's positioning now gives the COMPLETE bipartite/graph-aware comparator set — homogeneous bipartite (Lu et al. 2025), linear exposure-response MSE (Harshaw et al. 2021; Doudchenko et al. 2020), cluster-randomized (Brennan-Mirrokni-Pouget-Abadie 2022), and one-mode network precision (Viviano 2026) — and states what none recover (the graph-only conservative Hajek-variance envelope, its convex minimization, and the observable global KKT certificate). Do not alter any statement claim, construction formula, bound, proof, or conclusion.
